title: Ctenitis platyloba
intro: Ctenitis platyloba, also known as the Chinese wood fern or Chinese lady fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia, belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, headache, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitis platyloba include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal formulations such as capsules and tablets.
taxonomy: Ctenitis platyloba belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This species is a type of fern, specifically a member of the genus Ctenitis. Ctenitis platyloba is native to tropical Asia and is often found in forests and woodland areas. It is a relatively small fern with delicate fronds that are typically 20-50 cm long. The classification of Ctenitis platyloba is further divided into the class Polypodiopsida and the division Pteridophyta.
